summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a spirited Ventura show featuring an unforgettable Fire on the Mountain and a legendary second set.
review: The July 31‑1983 Ventura performance is praised for its high energy, tight jams and a standout Fire on the Mountain that many fans call “incredible.”  The Miller SBD delivers crystal‑clear audio – vocals and instruments are superbly defined with only occasional wind‑related hiss – making this one of the most listenable audience recordings of the era.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (9 out of 11) rate the show 4‑5 stars and highlight both excellent performance and superb soundboard quality; only one reviewer notes a negative performance issue.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear, crisp mix with super‑clear vocals and instruments; early part of the show has Jerry slightly low in the mix but balances out. Minor wind noise noted on an AUD comparison, but the SBD largely avoids it.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is described as up‑tempo and full of spirit, with a particularly strong second set that keeps the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Generally praised for his tone and clear presence; one reviewer notes he sounded a bit low early on, another claims he was 'stoned out' backstage.
